findings.summary: LM1117-3.3 detected as power regulator but topology='unknown' and vout=null despite value encoding voltage
findings.details: VREG1 (LM1117-3.3, lib: Regulator_Linear:LM1117-3.3) is correctly identified as a power_regulator but topology is 'unknown', estimated_vout is null, and input_rail/output_rail are both null. The component value 'LM1117-3.3' explicitly encodes the output voltage (3.3V) and the lib_id confirms it is a fixed linear regulator. The analyzer should parse the '-3.3' suffix and emit topology='fixed_linear' with estimated_vout=3.3. Additionally, pins=[] for VREG1 in the KiCad 5 legacy format means pin-to-net connectivity was not parsed, which is why input/output rails could not be traced — this is a secondary root cause.

findings.summary: LM1117-3.3 input (+5V) and output (+3.3V) rails not inferred despite power symbols present on same sheet
findings.details: Both +5V and +3.3V power rails are present on the sheet (visible in power_symbols and decoupling_analysis), and the design context clearly shows USB -> +5V -> LM1117-3.3 -> +3.3V. The analyzer emits decoupling_coverage observations for both +5V and +3.3V but fails to associate either rail with the regulator (input_rail=null, output_rail=null). Even without pin-level connectivity (pins=[] due to KiCad 5 legacy parsing), the analyzer could heuristically infer input/output rails by matching a fixed 3.3V regulator to the +5V and +3.3V rails present on the schematic.
